proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 (a continuous patient characteristic ranging 30-90, mean ~65, plausibly age in years) is positively correlated with pfs_months: higher feature_078 is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.pearsonr(df['feature_078'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = 0.856 between feature_078 and pfs_months (n=50000). Extremely strong positive linear relationship; higher feature_078 is associated with longer PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 (3-level integer 0/1/2, plausibly performance status) is negatively associated with pfs_months: higher levels imply shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Linear OLS coef = -1.16 per unit of feature_057 (mean PFS approx 4.7, 3.5, 2.3 across levels 0, 1, 2). Strongly negative monotone relationship.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 (continuous, range 0.12-3622, right-skewed lab value, plausibly PSA) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = -0.153, p < 1e-260. Higher feature_013 is associated with shorter PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 (continuous, range 0-24, mean 3.84, plausibly disease burden such as number of metastatic sites) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = -0.143, p < 1e-225. Higher feature_006 is associated with shorter PFS, consistent with a disease-burden interpretation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_009 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.80, plausibly serum albumin) is positively correlated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = +0.123, p < 1e-166. Higher feature_009 is associated with longer PFS, consistent with a positive prognostic biomarker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (binary, prevalence ~55%) is associated with shorter pfs_months: feature_051=1 patients have lower mean PFS than feature_051=0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 3.51 in feature_051=1 (n=27,481) vs 4.03 in feature_051=0 (n=22,519); difference -0.52 months, Welch t-test p < 1e-180. By far the strongest binary main effect on PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable linear model containing feature_078, feature_057, feature_013, feature_006, feature_009, and feature_051 jointly explains the majority of variance in pfs_months and each of these six variables retains an independent, signed effect in the predicted direction.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted R^2 = 0.946. All six predictors highly significant (p < 1e-100 each) with signed coefficients matching prior hypotheses: feature_078 +0.174 per unit, feature_057 -1.151 per level, feature_013 -0.0027 per unit, feature_006 -0.0764 per unit, feature_009 +0.479 per unit, feature_051 -0.376. These six features capture the great bulk of explainable variation in PFS in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across categories of feature_018 (race: white, black, hispanic, asian, other) — i.e., there is an unadjusted racial disparity in PFS.

analyses.result_summary: Group means: white 3.741, black 3.741, hispanic 3.737, asian 3.797, other 3.749. Overall ANOVA F(4, 49995) = 0.564, p = 0.69. No detectable unadjusted racial differences in PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across categories of feature_045 (insurance: private, medicare, medicaid, uninsured) — i.e., there is an unadjusted insurance-related disparity in PFS.

analyses.result_summary: Group means: private 3.736, medicare 3.754, medicaid 3.741, uninsured 3.726. Overall ANOVA F(3, 49996) = 0.344, p = 0.79. No detectable unadjusted insurance-related differences in PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for clinical baseline covariates (feature_078, feature_057, feature_013, feature_006, feature_009, feature_051), residual differences in pfs_months across feature_018 (race) categories remain non-zero.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted race coefficients (white reference): black -0.001 (p=0.90), hispanic +0.001 (p=0.92), asian +0.005 (p=0.55), other -0.003 (p=0.85). Overall partial F(4, 49989) = 0.114, p = 0.978. No racial disparity in PFS detectable after clinical adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for clinical baseline covariates, residual differences in pfs_months across feature_045 (insurance) categories remain non-zero.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted insurance coefficients (private reference): medicare +0.001 (p=0.85), medicaid -0.008 (p=0.22), uninsured ~0 (p=0.996). Overall partial F(3, 49990) = 0.66, p = 0.575. No insurance-related disparity in PFS detectable after clinical adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 (binary, prevalence ~10%, plausibly a treatment indicator) is associated with longer pfs_months independent of clinical baseline covariates.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for feature_109 = +0.156 months PFS, p = 6.1e-110. Patients with feature_109=1 have approximately 0.16 months longer PFS than feature_109=0 after adjusting for feature_078, feature_057, feature_013, feature_006, feature_009, feature_051.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_039 (binary, prevalence ~10%, plausibly a treatment indicator) is associated with longer pfs_months independent of clinical baseline covariates.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for feature_039 = +0.146 months PFS, p = 6.7e-99. Patients with feature_039=1 have approximately 0.15 months longer PFS than feature_039=0 after clinical adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 and feature_039 act synergistically: the combined effect of feature_109=1 AND feature_039=1 on pfs_months is substantially greater than the sum of their individual main effects (positive multiplicative interaction).

analyses.result_summary: After clinical adjustment: feature_109 main = +0.003 (p=0.63), feature_039 main = -0.003 (p=0.66), interaction feature_109:feature_039 = +1.440 (p ~ 0). Mean PFS by cell: 109=0,039=0: 3.74 (n=40,435); 109=1 alone: 3.67 (n=4,467); 039=1 alone: 3.67 (n=4,569); BOTH=1: 5.22 (n=529). The benefit emerges only when both are present — a strong positive interaction consistent with a combination-therapy effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The combination effect (feature_109=1 AND feature_039=1) on pfs_months is preserved within both strata of feature_051 (i.e., works regardless of feature_051 status).

analyses.code: stratified t-test of combo indicator within feature_051 strata
analyses.result_summary: feature_051=0: combo n=233, mean PFS 5.56 vs 4.02 in non-combo (diff +1.55, p=1.8e-13). feature_051=1: combo n=296, mean PFS 4.95 vs 3.49 in non-combo (diff +1.45, p=1.1e-14). The combination benefit is large and significant in both strata, supporting that the combo-treatment effect generalizes across feature_051 status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The magnitude of the combination treatment effect (both feature_109=1 and feature_039=1) on pfs_months differs across racial groups (feature_018) — i.e., differential benefit by race.

analyses.result_summary: Race-specific unadjusted combo effects: white +1.53 (p=1e-19), black +1.75 (p=2.5e-5), hispanic +0.99 (p=8e-3), asian +1.44 (p=0.04). Adjusted interaction terms (asian reference): white -0.18 (p=0.04), black -0.23 (p=0.21), hispanic -0.26 (p=0.007), other +0.29 (p=0.11). Hispanic patients show the smallest combo benefit; the contrast vs asian is nominally significant. With multiple comparisons across 4 race contrasts and modest n in subgroups, the evidence for genuine effect modification by race is weak — most patients across races derive a clinically large benefit (~1.0-1.7 months).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The magnitude of the combination treatment effect (both feature_109=1 and feature_039=1) on pfs_months differs across insurance categories (feature_045).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted combo:insurance interactions (medicaid reference, both main = +1.59): medicare -0.09 (p=0.13), private -0.29 (p<0.001), uninsured -0.04 (p=0.68). The combo benefit appears slightly attenuated in private-insurance vs medicaid patients (~1.30 vs 1.59 months) but absolute benefit remains large in all groups. Effect modification by insurance is at best weak.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is attenuated at higher levels of feature_057 (i.e., feature_051 x feature_057 interaction is positive in sign).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction coef = +0.028 per (feature_051 * feature_057) unit, p = 9e-6. Subgroup means: ECOG=0 feature_051 effect -0.56; ECOG=1 -0.51; ECOG=2 -0.47. Statistically significant but modest in absolute size; feature_051 effect remains substantially negative across all ECOG strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_051 on pfs_months becomes more pronounced (more negative) with higher feature_078 (age-like): feature_051 x feature_078 interaction coefficient is negative.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction coef = -0.0043 per (feature_051 * feature_078) unit, p ~ 1e-23. Combined with feature_051 main = -0.10, the effective penalty for feature_051=1 grows from ~0.30 months at feature_078=45 to ~0.49 months at feature_078=90. Statistically robust but modest in clinical size.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_013 (PSA-like) on pfs_months is attenuated when feature_051 = 1 (positive feature_013 x feature_051 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Main feature_013 coef = -0.0178 (when feature_051=0); feature_013:feature_051 interaction = +0.0153 (p ~ 0). Effective slope of PFS on feature_013 is -0.018 in feature_051=0 patients and approximately -0.0025 in feature_051=1. Strong, large-magnitude effect modification: feature_013 carries far more prognostic weight in feature_051=0 than feature_051=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_067 (5-level integer 6-10, plausibly Gleason score) is negatively associated with pfs_months after adjustment for clinical covariates.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coef = -0.011 per unit feature_067, p = 1.5e-7. Although unadjusted means are nearly flat across levels 6-10 (3.71, 3.76, 3.74, 3.74, 3.70), after adjustment a small but highly significant negative association is detectable. Clinically modest (~0.04 months over the full 6-10 range).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 (continuous, range 48-830) is negatively associated with pfs_months after adjustment for clinical covariates, despite weak unadjusted correlation.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coef = -0.00050 per unit, p = 4.7e-81 (unadjusted r = -0.017). After conditioning on the six-variable clinical model, a small but extremely robust negative effect appears: across the empirical range (~80-400), the predicted PFS difference is ~0.16 months. Suggests confounding by another covariate masks the unadjusted association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 (binary, prevalence ~60%) is positively associated with pfs_months after adjustment for clinical covariates.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coef = +0.022 months PFS for feature_027=1 vs 0, p = 3.0e-7. Small but statistically significant. Unadjusted t-test p was 0.27, indicating that this effect is uncovered only after clinical conditioning.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_043 (binary, prevalence ~20%) is negatively associated with pfs_months after adjustment for clinical covariates.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coef = -0.022 months PFS for feature_043=1 vs 0, p = 3.7e-5. Small magnitude but robust under adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Receipt of the favorable combination indicator (both feature_109=1 and feature_039=1) does not differ across racial categories (feature_018), so the lack of overall racial PFS disparity is not masking a treatment-access disparity that is offset by a differential biology.

analyses.result_summary: Combo prevalence: white 1.10%, black 0.96%, hispanic 1.03%, asian 0.99%, other 0.82%. Chi-square test across race x combo: chi2 = 1.76, p = 0.78. Combination treatment is delivered with effectively equal frequency across racial groups, supporting that PFS equality across race in this cohort is not an artifact of compensating disparities.
